mission.title: Install and Configure IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service (IKS)
mission.description: IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service (IKS) is a managed Kubernetes offering that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on IBM Cloud. It is ideal for users looking for a fully managed solution with integrated IBM Cloud services. Key trade-offs include reliance on IBM's infrastructure and potential costs associated with managed services.

steps.title: Install the IBM Cloud CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the IBM Cloud CLI, which is required to interact with IBM Cloud services.

```bash

curl -sL https://clis.cloud.ibm.com/install/linux.sh | bash

```

> This command downloads and installs the IBM Cloud CLI on Linux.

steps.title: Login to IBM Cloud
steps.description:
Authenticate your IBM Cloud account to manage resources.

```bash

ibmcloud login

```

> You will be prompted to enter your IBM Cloud credentials.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in a chosen region.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-create --name my-cluster --zone us-south-1 --kube-version 1.30 --num-nodes 3

```

> Replace 'my-cluster' with your desired cluster name.

steps.title: Set up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local environment to use the new Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-config --cluster my-cluster

```

> This command sets the KUBECONFIG environment variable.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Ensure that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Consider enabling autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-update --cluster my-cluster --enable-autoscale

ibmcloud ks monitoring enable --cluster my-cluster

```

> This command enables autoscaling for your cluster.
> This command enables monitoring for your cluster.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will remove the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-delete --cluster my-cluster --force

```

> Use --force to skip confirmation.

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
Check for and manually delete any orphaned resources such as load balancers or PVCs.

```bash

ibmcloud resource service-instance-list

```

> Review and delete any remaining resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. After upgrading, verify the cluster status.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-backup --cluster my-cluster

ibmcloud ks cluster-update --cluster my-cluster --kube-version 1.31

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup your cluster before proceeding.
> Upgrade to the latest Kubernetes version.
> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' status after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized' when running ibmcloud commands
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that your IBM Cloud CLI session has expired or is not authenticated.

**Fix:**
Re-run 'ibmcloud login' to authenticate again.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster not found' when trying to access the cluster
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified cluster name may be incorrect or the cluster may have been deleted.

**Fix:**
Check the cluster name and ensure it exists using 'ibmcloud ks clusters'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient quota' when creating a cluster
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Your IBM Cloud account may have reached its resource quota limits.

**Fix:**
Review your account quotas and delete unused resources or request a quota increase.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the kubeconfig is not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'ibmcloud ks cluster-config --cluster my-cluster' to set up kubeconfig correctly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30 features including new API versions and improved scheduling.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy APIs that may affect existing deployments.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Review the release notes for any necessary changes to your configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the IBM Cloud CLI and kubectl installed and configured.
